title: Mock Test 18 â€” Economics, Planning & Systems Engg.
pattern: GATE 2027 (65 Q Â· 100 marks Â· 3 hours)

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On a pie chart of $800$ students, one sector spans $45^\circ$. Compute the number of students in that sector.

figure.caption: Pie sector of 45 degrees
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 127 38 Z' fill='#16a34a'/><text x='22' y='160' font-size='8'>sector 45 deg, total 800</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count $=\dfrac{\theta}{360}\times\text{total}$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{45}{360}\times800=100$$

**Answer.** $100$ students.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is she related to the man?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve the chain from the innermost phrase.

**Working.** 'My grandfather's only son' is the man's father; his daughter is the man's sister.

**Answer.** Sister.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The line graph shows a value rising from $20$ to $50$. Compute the percentage increase (%).

figure.caption: Line graph
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><polyline points='40,90 100,75 160,40' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='40' cy='90' r='3' fill='#dc2626'/><circle cx='160' cy='40' r='3' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='45' y='138' font-size='8'>20 -> 50</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\%\text{ increase}=\dfrac{\Delta}{\text{initial}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{50-20}{20}\times100=\frac{30}{20}\times100=150\%$$

**Answer.** $150\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points to the right (east). After rotating it $90^\circ$ clockwise, it points

figure.caption: A horizontal arrow pointing to the right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='120' y2='40' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='120,40 105,32 105,48' fill='#6366f1'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $90^\circ$ clockwise turn sends east to south.

**Working.** Right rotated clockwise becomes downward.

**Answer.** Downward (south).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Question: Is $x>10$? (I) $x$ is a positive even number. (II) $x=12$. Which is sufficient?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A statement is sufficient if it answers the question definitively.

**Working.** I allows $x=2$ (no) or $x=12$ (yes), so it is inconclusive; II fixes $x=12>10$.

**Answer.** II alone is sufficient.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A price is first increased by $10\%$ and then decreased by $10\%$. The net percentage change in the price is _____ % (use a negative sign for a decrease).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combine successive factors multiplicatively.

**Working.**
$$1.10\times 0.90 = 0.99 \Rightarrow -1\%$$

**Answer.** $-1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude â€” Boats and Streams
questions.stem: A boat covers $16$ km downstream in $2$ hours and returns the same distance upstream in $4$ hours. The speed of the boat in still water is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Boat speed = (downstream + upstream)/2.

**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{16}{2}=8$, upstream $=\tfrac{16}{4}=4$ km/h; boat $=\dfrac{8+4}{2}=6$ km/h.

**Answer.** $6$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert to the passive voice: 'The committee approved the safety proposal.'
options: The safety proposal was approved by the committee.
The safety proposal is approved by the committee.
The safety proposal has approved the committee.
The committee was approved by the safety proposal.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Passive voice: object becomes subject, verb becomes 'be + past participle', and the tense is preserved.

**Working.** Simple past 'approved' becomes 'was approved'; the object 'proposal' moves to the front.

**Answer.** 'The safety proposal was approved by the committee.'

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: All miners are workers. All workers are humans. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Transitivity of universal affirmatives.

**Working.** Miners âŠ† workers âŠ† humans, so all miners are humans.

**Answer.** All miners are humans.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The volume of a sphere of radius $3$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$, round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume of a sphere $=\tfrac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.

**Working.** $\tfrac{4}{3}\times\dfrac{22}{7}\times 27 = \dfrac{2376}{21}\approx 113.14$.

**Answer.** $\approx 113.14$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a perfectly symmetric normal distribution, the relationship between mean, median and mode is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Symmetry of the normal curve.

**Working.** All three measures of central tendency coincide at the centre.

**Answer.** Mean $=$ median $=$ mode.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The principal source of heat in a deep mine is usually
options: the geothermal heat of the surrounding rock (rising with the geothermal gradient), supplemented by autocompression and machinery
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rock heat dominates at depth.

**Reasoning.** Strata temperature rises with depth (geothermal gradient), and air heats by autocompression as it descends; machinery, oxidation, men and blasting add further heat. Rock heat is usually the largest contributor in deep mines.

**Answer.** Geothermal rock heat (plus autocompression/machinery).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The standard deviation of the data $4,\,4,\,4,\,4$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Standard deviation measures spread about the mean.

**Working.** All values equal the mean ($4$), so every deviation is zero.
$$\sigma=0$$

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Offsets 0, 2, 4, 3, 0 m are taken at a regular interval of 10 m. By the trapezoidal rule the enclosed area, in mÂ², is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $A=d\left[\dfrac{o_1+o_n}{2}+\sum o_{mid}\right]$.

**Working.**
$$A=10\left[\frac{0+0}{2}+(2+4+3)\right]=10\times9=90\ \text{m}^2$$

**Answer.** 90 mÂ².

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: A tacheometer with multiplying constant $k=100$ and additive constant $c=0$ reads a staff intercept $s=0.500$ m on a horizontal sight. Find the horizontal distance (m).

figure.caption: Stadia tacheometry
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='160' y='30' width='10' height='100' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='160' y1='50' x2='170' y2='50' stroke='#dc2626'/><line x1='160' y1='110' x2='170' y2='110' stroke='#dc2626'/><circle cx='40' cy='80' r='12' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#b45309'/><line x1='52' y1='80' x2='160' y2='80' stroke='#475569' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='28' y='146' font-size='8'>k=100, c=0, s=0.5 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Horizontal distance $D=k\,s+c$ for a horizontal stadia sight.

**Working.**
$$D=100\times0.500+0=50\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $50$ m.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: At the EOQ ($20$ orders/year, $S=\textsf{Rs}\,50$), the annual ordering cost is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ordering cost $=\dfrac{D}{Q}\,S$.

**Working.**
$$20\times50=1000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,1000$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A reserve of $1$ crore ($10{,}000{,}000$) tonnes is mined at $10{,}00{,}000$ tonnes per year. The mine life is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mine life $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{10000000}{1000000}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10$ years.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A binomial random variable has $n=5,\;p=0.2$. Its mean is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mean $=np$.

**Working.**
$$\mu=5\times 0.2=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: If the normal reaction is $200\,\text{N}$ and $\mu=0.25$, the limiting friction force is _____ N.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $F=\mu N$.

**Working.**
$$F=0.25\times 200=50.$$

**Answer.** $50\,\text{N}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The large block of mineral left unworked around the shaft in the plan shown is a:
options: shaft (safety) pillar protecting shaft from subsidence

figure.caption: Shaft pillar
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='180' fill='#e5e7eb'/><circle cx='100' cy='100' r='55' fill='#1f2937'/><circle cx='100' cy='100' r='10' fill='#fff' stroke='#475569'/><text x='84' y='80' font-size='8' fill='#fff'>shaft pillar</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A shaft pillar is unworked ground left to protect the shaft.

**Reasoning.** The ring of solid mineral around the shaft prevents subsidence damage to the shaft and surface installations; it is the shaft (safety) pillar.

**Answer.** Shaft (safety) pillar.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A slope face dips $45^\circ$ and a joint dips $30^\circ$ in the same direction; the friction angle is $20^\circ$. For this joint, plane sliding is
options: kinematically feasible (joint daylights and dips steeper than $\phi$)
impossible because the joint dips into the slope
impossible because the joint is flatter than $\phi$

figure.caption: Stereonet: slope face (dip 45 deg) and a joint (dip 30 deg) in the same direction.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Need $\phi<\psi_p<\psi_f$.

**Working.** Here $20^\circ<30^\circ<45^\circ$, so the joint daylights and exceeds friction.

**Answer.** kinematically feasible.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: With $FC=\textsf{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ and $VC=\textsf{Rs}\,60$/unit, the total cost of producing $10{,}000$ units is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A point source gives $96\ \text{dB}$ at $1\ \text{m}$. Assuming free-field spherical spreading, compute the level at $2\ \text{m}$ (dB).

figure.caption: Inverse-square decay of sound
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='16' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='100' cy='60' r='3' fill='#1f2937'/><circle cx='170' cy='60' r='3' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='26' y='64' font-size='7'>src</text><text x='88' y='50' font-size='8'>1 m</text><text x='150' y='50' font-size='8'>2 m</text><text x='28' y='100' font-size='8'>96 dB at 1 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each doubling of distance reduces the level by $6\ \text{dB}$ ($20\log_{10}2$).

**Working.**
$$L=96-6=90\ \text{dB}$$

**Answer.** $90$ dB.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A $2\times2$ matrix with a repeated eigenvalue $\lambda$ is diagonalizable if and only if
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A matrix is diagonalizable iff the geometric multiplicity equals the algebraic multiplicity for every eigenvalue.

**Working.** With a single repeated eigenvalue $\lambda$ (algebraic multiplicity $2$), diagonalizability needs two independent eigenvectors, i.e. $A-\lambda I=0$, so $A=\lambda I$. (Any $2\times2$ matrix has $\det=\lambda^{2}$ for a repeated eigenvalue, so that condition is automatic and insufficient.)

**Answer.** $A=\lambda I$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A closed traverse of perimeter 1000 m has a linear closing error of 0.50 m. Its relative error of closure expressed as 1 in n gives n = ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Relative error $=\dfrac{e}{\text{perimeter}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{0.50}{1000}=\frac{1}{2000}\Rightarrow 1\text{ in }2000$$

**Answer.** n = 2000.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.topic: Engineering Mechanics â€” Resultant of Forces
questions.stem: Two forces of $3\,\text{N}$ and $4\,\text{N}$ act at a point at right angles to each other. The magnitude of their resultant is _____ N.

figure.caption: Two perpendicular forces of 3 N and 4 N acting at a point.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='130' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='130,120 116,114 116,126' fill='#6366f1'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='30' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='30,30 24,44 36,44' fill='#10b981'/><text x='95' y='138' font-size='12' fill='#6366f1'>4 N</text><text x='6' y='75' font-size='12' fill='#10b981'>3 N</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For perpendicular forces, $R=\sqrt{F_1^2+F_2^2}$.

**Working.**
$$R=\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=\sqrt{25}=5.$$

**Answer.** $5\,\text{N}$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: The difference between a mineral 'reserve' and a mineral 'resource' is that a reserve is
options: the economically and legally mineable part of a resource under current conditions
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Reserves are the economic subset of resources.

**Reasoning.** A resource is a concentration with reasonable prospects of eventual economic extraction; a reserve is the portion that is demonstrably economic and mineable now (modifying factors applied).

**Answer.** Economically mineable part of the resource.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money â€” Compound Interest
questions.stem: The compound interest earned on $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$ at $8\%$ per year for $3$ years is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bench block has an in-situ volume of 1000 mÂ³ and the rock density is 2.5 t/mÂ³. The tonnage, in t, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ in-situ volume $\times$ bank density.

**Working.**
$$T=1000\times2.5=2500\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** 2500 t.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: If each of the 40 holes breaks 250 mÂ³, the total rock volume broken by the round, in mÂ³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total volume $=$ holes $\times$ volume per hole.

**Working.**
$$V=40\times250=10000\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 10000 mÂ³.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The bar chart shows permissible exposure decreasing as noise level rises. Under the $3\ \text{dB}$ exchange rate, each $3\ \text{dB}$ increase in level:
options: increases the permissible time by 3 hours

figure.caption: Exchange rate for noise exposure
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='180' y2='110' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='50' y='80' width='14' height='30' fill='#94a3b8'/><rect x='90' y='55' width='14' height='55' fill='#94a3b8'/><rect x='130' y='35' width='14' height='75' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='40' y='128' font-size='8'>+3 dB halves exposure</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The exchange rate links an increase in level to a reduction in allowed time at equal noise dose.

**Reasoning.** With a $3\ \text{dB}$ exchange rate the sound energy doubles for every $3\ \text{dB}$, so the permissible exposure time is halved.

**Answer.** It halves the permissible exposure time.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: An ore block has a volume of $5{,}00{,}000\,\text{m}^3$ and a bulk density of $2.7\,\text{t/m}^3$. Its tonnage is _____ tonnes.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ volume $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$500000\times2.7=1350000.$$

**Answer.** $13{,}50{,}000$ tonnes.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The critical path consists of three activities of duration $5$, $8$ and $7$ days. The project duration is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Project duration $=$ sum of critical activity times.

**Working.**
$$5+8+7=20.$$

**Answer.** $20$ days.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Mohs scale of hardness is a relative scale running from
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohs scale ranks scratch hardness.

**Reasoning.** It is an ordinal scale from talc (1, softest) to diamond (10, hardest); a higher mineral scratches a lower one. It is relative, not proportional.

**Answer.** 1 (talc) to 10 (diamond).

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In shaft hoisting, a skip is used mainly to
options: transport men and materials in a floored container
hoist bulk mineral, discharged by tipping or a bottom door
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Skips are bulk-handling conveyances.

**Reasoning.** A skip is a steel box that loads ore/waste at a measuring pocket and discharges it automatically at the top; men, materials and mine cars are carried in cages instead.

**Answer.** Hoisting bulk mineral.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Groundwater enters a pit sump at 500 mÂ³/h and the pump removes 600 mÂ³/h. The net rate at which the sump is dewatered, in mÂ³/h, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net dewatering $=$ pumping rate $-$ inflow.

**Working.**
$$\dot V=600-500=100\ \text{m}^3/\text{h}$$

**Answer.** 100 mÂ³/h.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: In order of increasing geological confidence, mineral resources are classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Confidence rises from inferred to measured.

**Working.** The correct increasing order is inferred $\to$ indicated $\to$ measured.

**Answer.** inferred, indicated, measured.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: For a pillar strength of 13.22 MPa and a pillar stress of 7.81 MPa, the factor of safety is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $FS=\dfrac{\text{pillar strength}}{\text{pillar stress}}$.

**Working.**
$$FS=\frac{13.22}{7.81}=1.69$$

**Answer.** 1.69.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the same opening ($\sigma_v=10$, $\sigma_h=0\,\text{MPa}$), the tangential stress at the crown (roof) is $3\sigma_h-\sigma_v$, i.e. _____ MPa (a negative value indicates tension).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Kirsch crown stress: $\sigma_\theta=3\sigma_h-\sigma_v$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_\theta=3(0)-10=-10.$$

**Answer.** $-10\,\text{MPa}$ (tensile).

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: The Weisbach triangle method of shaft correlation places the theodolite
options: exactly on the line of the two plumb wires
slightly off the line joining the two wires, forming a long, narrow triangle with a very small angle at the instrument
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A small offset angle is measured precisely.

**Reasoning.** Because the instrument cannot sit exactly on the wire line, it is set a little off, forming a very acute triangle; the tiny angle at the instrument is computed by the sine rule to bring the underground line onto the wire-plane direction.

**Answer.** Slightly off the wire line (narrow triangle).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money â€” Continuous Discounting
questions.stem: The present worth of $\textsf{Rs}\,20{,}000$ due in $3$ years at $8\%$ compounded continuously is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to the nearest rupee.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock has $c=2\,\text{MPa}$ and $\phi=25^\circ$. The shear strength on a plane with $\sigma_n=30\,\text{MPa}$ is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau=c+\sigma_n\tan\phi$.

**Working.**
$$\tau=2+30\tan 25^\circ=2+30(0.4663)=2+13.99=15.99.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 15.99\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan has a static pressure of $480\,\text{Pa}$ and a velocity pressure of $20\,\text{Pa}$. Its total pressure is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fan total pressure $=$ static $+$ velocity pressure.

**Working.**
$$480+20=500.$$

**Answer.** $500\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: For the $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$ asset (DDB, rate $40\%$), the depreciation charged in year $2$ is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: For the bench blast geometry shown, with burden 4.5 m and spacing 5.6 m, the plan area broken per hole (B Ã— S), in mÂ², is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hole is responsible for a plan rectangle of burden Ã— spacing.

**Working.**
$$A=B\times S=4.5\times5.6=25.2\ \text{m}^2$$

**Answer.** 25.2 mÂ².

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For most rock slopes, the least favourable (most critical) groundwater condition is
options: fully saturated with high pore-water pressure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pore pressure reduces effective normal stress and shear strength.

**Working.** Full saturation gives the highest uplift/water thrust and lowest FoS.

**Answer.** fully saturated with high pore-water pressure.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money â€” Loan Amortization
questions.stem: A loan of $\textsf{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ at $10\%$ is repaid in $4$ equal year-end installments. Each installment is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to the nearest rupee.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: In dragline stripping, 'rehandle' refers to
options: spoil that must be moved a second time, reducing the dragline's effective (prime) output
the first dig of fresh overburden
the maintenance time of the dragline
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Limited reach forces some spoil to be cast twice.

**Reasoning.** When the void cannot accept all spoil in one cast, material is placed temporarily and later re-excavated and recast â€” rehandle â€” which consumes dragline cycles without removing new overburden, lowering effective production.

**Answer.** Spoil moved a second time, reducing prime output.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A round uses 4800 kg of explosive at Rs 50/kg to break 25000 t of rock. The explosive cost, in Rs per tonne, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Unit cost $=\dfrac{\text{total explosive cost}}{\text{tonnes broken}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{Cost}=4800\times50=\text{Rs }240000,\quad \frac{240000}{25000}=9.6\ \text{Rs/t}$$

**Answer.** Rs 9.6 per tonne.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Mining $20{,}00{,}000$ tonnes of ore at $\textsf{Rs}\,100$/t costs $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cost $=$ tonnage $\times$ unit cost.

**Working.**
$$2000000\times100=200000000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,20$ crore.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A project has present-worth benefits of $\textsf{Rs}\,2{,}50{,}000$, an initial cost of $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}50{,}000$ and present-worth O&M of $\textsf{Rs}\,50{,}000$. The benefit-cost ratio is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $B/C=\dfrac{\text{PW benefits}}{\text{PW total costs}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{250000}{150000+50000}=\frac{250000}{200000}=1.25.$$

**Answer.** $1.25$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.topic: Engineering Mechanics â€” Moment of Inertia
questions.stem: The parallel-axis theorem expresses the moment of inertia about a parallel axis as $I=I_c+\;?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shifting the reference axis adds $Ad^2$.

**Working.** $I=I_c+Ad^2$, where $d$ is the separation between the axes.

**Answer.** $A d^2$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For a given fan running at variable speed $N$, the power absorbed varies as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Third fan law.

**Working.** Power is proportional to the cube of speed.

**Answer.** $W\propto N^3$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: An activity has Earliest Start $=4$ and Latest Start $=7$ days. Its total float is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total float $=$ LS $-$ ES.

**Working.**
$$7-4=3.$$

**Answer.** $3$ days.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Depreciation â€” Salvage from Book Value
questions.stem: An asset of $\textsf{Rs}\,6{,}00{,}000$ is fully straight-line depreciated at $\textsf{Rs}\,55{,}000$/year over $10$ years. Its book value (equal to salvage) at end of life is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: For the $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$ asset (DDB, rate $40\%$), the book value at the end of year $2$ is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: For a staggered pattern with spacing = 1.25 Ã— burden and a burden of 4.5 m, the spacing, in m, is ____ (two decimals).

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Differential GPS (DGPS / RTK) improves positioning accuracy by
options: using a reference (base) station at a known point to compute and broadcast corrections that the rover applies
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Correlated errors cancel between nearby receivers.

**Reasoning.** A base on a known point determines the range errors and sends corrections to the rover; common errors (orbit, clock, atmosphere) largely cancel, giving cmâ€“dm accuracy (RTK to cm).

**Answer.** Base-station corrections to the rover.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Mine Planning â€” Break-Even Stripping Ratio
questions.stem: Ore yields an NSR of $\textsf{Rs}\,7200$/t against an ore cost of $\textsf{Rs}\,5000$/t; waste removal costs $\textsf{Rs}\,1100$/t. The break-even stripping ratio is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{BESR}=\dfrac{\text{NSR}-\text{ore cost}}{\text{waste cost}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{BESR}=\frac{7200-5000}{1100}=\frac{2200}{1100}=2.0.$$

**Answer.** $2.0$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: An emulsion explosive is essentially
options: a water-in-oil emulsion of fine oxidiser droplets, giving excellent water resistance
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Emulsions are oxidiser droplets dispersed in oil.

**Reasoning.** Microscopic ammonium-nitrate solution droplets coated by an oil/fuel phase give intimate oxidiser-fuel contact, high density and very good water resistance â€” ideal for wet holes; sensitised by gas bubbles or microballoons.

**Answer.** Water-in-oil emulsion (water resistant).

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: For the closed traverse shown, the algebraic sums of latitudes and departures are $\sum L=+0.3$ m and $\sum D=-0.4$ m. The linear closing error, in m, is _____ (two decimals).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,160 160,160 180,80 90,40 30,90' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='40' cy='160' r='3'/><circle cx='160' cy='160' r='3'/><circle cx='180' cy='80' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='40' r='3'/><circle cx='30' cy='90' r='3'/></g><text x='32' y='176' font-size='10'>A</text><text x='158' y='176' font-size='10'>B</text><text x='186' y='82' font-size='10'>C</text><text x='86' y='34' font-size='10'>D</text><text x='14' y='92' font-size='10'>E</text></svg>
figure.caption: Closed traverse ABCDE

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Closing error $e=\sqrt{(\sum L)^2+(\sum D)^2}$.

**Working.**
$$e=\sqrt{0.3^2+(-0.4)^2}=\sqrt{0.09+0.16}=\sqrt{0.25}=0.5\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $0.5$ m.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A major advantage of pumped/augered bulk explosives (ANFO, bulk emulsion) over cartridged explosives is that they
options: completely fill the borehole cross-section (good coupling), load quickly and cost less per kg
cannot be used in large holes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bulk loading fully couples the charge.

**Reasoning.** Bulk products fill the whole hole diameter, maximising coupling and energy transfer, are loaded fast by truck, and are cheaper than packaged cartridges â€” hence dominant in large opencast operations.

**Answer.** Full coupling, fast loading, lower cost.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Calcite, the chief mineral of limestone, can be readily identified because it
options: effervesces (fizzes) with cold dilute hydrochloric acid and has a hardness of 3 with rhombohedral cleavage
shows conchoidal fracture and no cleavage
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Carbonates react with acid.

**Reasoning.** Calcite (CaCOâ‚ƒ) releases COâ‚‚ with dilute HCl; it is hardness 3, shows perfect rhombohedral cleavage and strong double refraction.

**Answer.** Effervesces with dilute HCl, hardness 3.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The flammable (explosive) range of methane in air is approximately
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Methane explodes within fixed concentration limits.

**Working.** The range is roughly $5\%$ (lower) to $15\%$ (upper), most violent near $9.5\%$.

**Answer.** 5% to 15%.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Investment Analysis â€” After-Tax Cash Flow
questions.stem: A mine has revenue minus operating cost of $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}80{,}000$, depreciation of $\textsf{Rs}\,45{,}000$ and a tax rate of $30\%$. The after-tax cash flow is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
